title: Extreme heat disrupting work and sleep for residents of African informal settlements
content: A quarter of people in three African neighbourhoods have been forced to take time off work due to heat stress, research by IIED and Slum Dwellers International reveals.

title: Heat impacts on informal settlements: participatory heat data collection in Kenya, Tanzania and Zimbabwe
content: This issue paper reports the findings of pilot participatory heat data collection in Kenya, Tanzania and Zimbabwe.

title: Livable Pacific cities and towns: Spotlight: Tides of change - Urban resilience across Pacific landscapes
content: The Livable Pacific Cities and Towns report sets out a strategic framework for resilient urbanization, built around four mutually reinforcing building blocks.

title: Livable Pacific cities and towns: Spotlight: Mapping risk, building resilience: Exposure analysis of Pacific urban areas
content: This Spotlight details the results from risk modelling and scientific analyses designed by World Bank staff to identify the exposure at risk (EaR) from critical natural hazards in 38 urban areas in 10 Pacific Island Countries (PICs).

title: South Africa: Making sense of a water crisis after historic floods
content: The City of Johannesburg, South Africa’s economic capital and most densely populated city, is experiencing a water shortage which has seen residents bathing in public in protest.

title: Compilation on RVA leading to resilience of bridges
content: This paper presents the results of RVA conducted to support informed decision-making, prioritization of interventions, and enhancement of long-term resilience of bridge infrastructure in Kolhapur District.

title: Wildfire disasters avoided in Chile
content: Technical experts at Chile’s National Forestry Agency, have been documenting avoided loses due to wildfires since 2014-2015. Their goal is to use these analyses to facilitate the assessment of prevention and response actions and improve future policies.

title: Verture
content: Verture is a Scottish climate resilience charity that aims to help people thrive as the climate changes by creating connections, actions and insights for a fairer and smarter future.

title: In a heatwave, a cool library or shopping centre is a lifeline. Do we need more climate shelters?
content: As public spaces like libraries and malls become vital refuges during extreme heat, the need for formal "climate shelters" is rising. The article evaluates if current infrastructure provides enough protection and accessibility for vulnerable populations.

title: Building climate-resilient social care: Guidance for practitioners
content: This document provides practical guidance for assessing and addressing climate-related risks such as heat, flooding, and extreme weather to ensure continuity of social care service.

title: Identifying information voids during weather-related diasters: Case studies from the 2024 Europe floods and Florida’s hurricane helene
content: This paper conceptualizes and tests a novel survey tool designed to detect information voids across four key dimensions: information quantity, quality, source, and channel.

title: Call for expressions of interest for IRDR International Centres of Excellence
content: Integrated Research on Disaster Risk (IRDR), a global scientific programme, invites applications from qualified institutes to become IRDR International Centres of Excellence (IRDR ICoEs).

title: Urbanization and Local-scale storm characteristics dominate spatially divergent tropical cyclone rainfall trends in South China
content: This article examines how urbanization and local-scale storm characteristics shape tropical cyclone rainfall trends in South China, challenging the common assumption that climate warming leads to uniform increases in storm-related precipitation.

title: Wildfire smoke increases assaults: Evidence from Seattle
content: This study highlights the direct causal link between wildfire-derived air pollution and interpersonal violence in urban environments, focusing on Seattle, Washington, between 2013 and 2023.

title: Continental drying: A threat to our common future
content: This report outlines the accelerating decline in global freshwater availability, described as continental drying, and presents new evidence on where, why, and how this trend is unfolding.

title: WMO Hurricane Committee retires name of Melissa, replaces it with Molly
content: Hurricane Melissa’s name was retired after its catastrophic 2025 impact in the Caribbean, causing 90+ deaths and record‑strength landfall in Jamaica; it will be replaced by Molly.

title: Arizona’s water is drying up. That’s not stopping the data center rush
content: Though tech companies are secretive about their water usage, Arizona’s 150-plus data centers and chip factories use a tiny fraction of the state’s supply.

title: After a lawsuit, USDA agrees to share climate risk data with farmers
content: Εmployees at the U.S. Department of Agriculture were reportedly instructed to flag and delete any webpages that mentioned climate change — including resources used by farmers to prepare for extreme weather.

title: Some people are getting trapped in place as the climate changes
content: Research shows that the poorest may be least able to move away from harm.

title: Sea level worldwide higher than previously assumed
content: An international analysis has been published in Nature showing that actual sea levels are significantly higher than previously assumed in most studies of the consequences for coastal areas.

title: Satellite study of 2.2 million thunderstorms shows how to predict their formation
content: Thunderstorms form more predictably than thought: soil‑moisture patterns aligning with wind shear help pinpoint where storms will rapidly grow, improving early warnings and forecasting on hot days.

title: From FitzRoy's barometers to impact-based phone alerts: How weather warnings have evolved across the UK and Ireland
content: Tracing the history of UK and Irish weather alerts, this article explores the shift from FitzRoy’s 1860s barometers and visual signals to modern, impact-based phone alerts and Met Éireann’s move toward precise, polygon-based warning areas.

title: Netweather
content: Netweather is one of the leading independent weather forecasting companies in the UK.

title: A lesson in vulnerability: How Nevado del Ruiz changed risk disaster management
content: The article argues that the 1985 eruption of the Nevado del Ruiz volcano, which killed more than 23,000 people largely due to poor preparedness and miscommunication, became a turning point that highlighted the role of vulnerability in disasters.

title: Grant support for Strengthening Early Warning and Early Action System for Meteorological, Hydrological, and Climate Extremes in Lao PDR 2025–2029
content: UNDRR invites non-profit organisations to submit proposals for grant support for Strengthening Early Warning and Early Action System for Meteorological, Hydrological, and Climate Extremes in Lao PDR 2025–2029.

title: How cyclone Winston affected people and what we learnt from it
content: Cyclone Winston devastated Fiji in 2016, destroying homes, disrupting livelihoods, and exposing weaknesses in infrastructure, preparedness, and communication. Reforms since then have reduced risks.

title: Uncovering flood-driven deprivation from space
content: Floods in Bangladesh repeatedly damage livelihoods and deepen long‑term deprivation; satellite data helps identify the most exposed and vulnerable areas to better target adaptation and reduce poverty cycles.

title: Turning vision into action: Advancing the implementation of climate strategies through effective institutions
content: This text explains that disasters should be understood as complex systems made up of interconnected factors, not just the triggering hazard itself.

title: Seoul, Korea helps avoid flood disasters
content: An elevated expressway was turned into the Cheonggyecheon stream. Riverside paths are closed in heavy rain and are social spaces at other times. Air quality improved and traffic reduced, but the project remains controversial.

title: Managing flooding in Viet Nam
content: Good national to local government links exist, with good governance a key focus. Earth observations provide data and forecasting for people to take proactive action. Local community groups ensure key information and data are reviewed and acted upon.

title: Wildfire management in Australia
content: Wildfire disasters are being avoided in Australia because many groups of people are coordinating actions and working towards a common goal. Government, communities and businesses are investing in wildfire risk reduction.

title: Managing flooding in Bangladesh
content: Village communities work with NGOs to build dwellings that can cope with flooding. Earth observations data is used for decision-making at national, state and local levels. Tested communications channels alert and warn people, who act on the advice.

title: Managing flooding in Hamburg's HafenCity
content: Good government regulation and urban planning lead to good urban resilience. Multiple tested communications channels alert and warn citizens, who act on the advice of authorities because of meaningful inclusion and respect.

title: Making space for the Dutch River Ijssel
content: Unexpected flooding incited a programme on Dutch rivers to rehabilitate rivers while making them safer from floods. The city of Deventer took its time and embarked on an extensive consultative and cooperative process.

title: After a hurricane, extreme heat poses a serious threat to recovery workers
content: Research on Hurricane Beryl shows recovery workers face extreme, often overlooked heat risk, worsened by power outages and climate change. Tailored safety measures and staffing are needed to protect crews.

title: Global fertility responses to climate-related hazards depend on population disruption, lethality, and hazard type
content: This paper combines global fertility data with disaster records for 1950–2023 to estimate fertility responses to climate-related hazards, distinguishing between population disruption (affected-rate exposure) and lethality (death-rate exposure).

title: Toward a standard for landslide data: Bridging gaps in landslide susceptibility modeling and early warning systems
content: This report outlines the urgent need for a unified global standard for landslide data to strengthen disaster risk reduction, particularly in susceptibility modelling and early warning systems.

title: Seminar: From disaster early warning to early action
content: This seminar explores how we can move from early warning to early action. The Wageningen Disaster Network joins forces with the NWO-DST Merian LODESTAR project to host this timely discussion.

title: Wet winter, hot summer? What ‘climate whiplash’ means for the UK
content: Climate whiplash describes rapid swings from one type of weather extreme to another, most commonly from really persistent drought to really persistent wet weather.

title: Future hotspots of hazardous rivers in the atmosphere
content: Atmospheric rivers are becoming more frequent and intense, especially in the North Pacific, North Atlantic, Southern Ocean, and parts of the U.S. and Europe, increasing risks of heavy precipitation, snowfall, warm winters, and extreme heat.

title: How Urban Planning faculty turned a historic flood into a living policy lab
content: Urban planning students at UW–Milwaukee turned the August 2025 Milwaukee flood into a real‑time policy lab, partnering with the sewerage district to improve its program that reduces basement backups.

title: Too hot to handle? Building resilience to extreme heat in the private sector
content: Gaining a better understanding of emerging risks, such as extreme heat, allows us to gather insights about the effects they will have on businesses, and the insurance coverage they will likely need in future.

title: 球磨村 (熊本県）　Kuma Village
content: 球磨村（くまむら）は、熊本県の南部にある村。Kuma is a village located in Kuma District, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan.
